political_stances.stance_content: Current state of private insurance and private healthcare is unsustainable. Policy changes introduced to moderate private healthcare cost escalation and premium escalation for Integrated Shield Plan riders.
Government plans to release land for new not-for-profit private hospital in eastern Singapore, first in almost 20 years, to provide lower-cost private healthcare options.
Healthcare manpower will be a key agenda in the coming years as Singapore expands and transforms its workforce to meet the needs of a growing, ageing population.
Moved bill in Parliament to confer the Singapore University of Social Sciences autonomous status.

major_achievements.achievement: Deputy Chief Negotiator for the Singapore-United States Free Trade Agreement
Served as CEO of WDA, overseeing workforce development programmes
Co-chaired the COVID-19 Multi-Ministry Taskforce managing Singapore's pandemic response
Appointed Minister for Health, overseeing Singapore's healthcare transformation
Appointed Treasurer of PAP and Co-ordinating Minister for Economic and Social Policies

biography_summary: Ong Ye Kung is a Singaporean politician and former civil servant who has served as Treasurer of the PAP since 2025 and Minister for Health since 2021. His father, Ong Lian Teng, was a Barisan Sosialis politician. Ong was Principal Private Secretary to Deputy PM Lee Hsien Loong (2002-2004), CEO of the Workforce Development Agency (2005-2008), and held positions at NTUC and Keppel Corporation. He lost in Aljunied GRC in the 2011 election before winning Sembawang GRC in 2015. He was considered a leading 4G leadership candidate and co-chaired the COVID-19 taskforce.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with family members, political stances on healthcare/education, major achievements, person relationships from Wikipedia and web search
